flashcards.back: A blockchain fork is a split in the blockchain protocol where the network diverges into two separate paths, either temporarily or permanently.
An accidental fork occurs when two miners solve a block almost simultaneously, creating two competing chains until the network resolves it by adopting the longest chain.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a soft fork and a hard fork?
flashcards.back: A soft fork is a backward-compatible upgrade that tightens rules, while a hard fork is a non-backward-compatible change that creates a permanent split in the blockchain.
Orphan blocks are valid blocks that are not part of the longest chain and are discarded when the network resolves an accidental fork.

flashcards.front: Give an example of a hard fork.
flashcards.back: Bitcoin Cash splitting from Bitcoin in 2017 is a prominent example of a hard fork, creating two separate cryptocurrencies.
